claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 6 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 6 (under 16)
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£0.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child in hospital for 13 weeks but claimant regularly spends on child
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£0.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
circumstances: Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£0.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
Child is not in local authority care
Child in hospital for 13 weeks but claimant regularly spends on child
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. 

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances. Let me start by reading the official guidance on Child Benefit eligibility.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. Now I understand the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit. Let me ask you the first question:

**Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you asking about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do have a child. I'm asking about my son Alex - he's 6 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Now I need to understand a bit more about your and Alex's situation.

**Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. Now I need to understand your relationship with Alex:

**Does Alex live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't live with me at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. Since Alex doesn't live with you currently, I need to understand more about the situation.

**Who does Alex live with, and do you contribute financially towards his upkeep (such as for food, clothes, pocket money, or accommodation)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex is actually in hospital at the moment. And yes, I do still spend money on him - I buy him clothes, toys, magazines, things like that to keep him comfortable while he's there.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for sharing that. I'm sorry to hear Alex is in hospital. I need to understand the timeline to assess your eligibility properly.

**How long has Alex been in hospital?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He's been there for about 3 months now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I have a few more questions to fully assess your eligibility.

**Before Alex went into hospital, did he live with you? And do you continue to regularly spend money on him while he's in hospital (as you mentioned with the clothes, toys, and magazines)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he lived with me before he went into hospital. And yes, I do continue to regularly spend money on him - I buy him clothes, toys, magazines, and other things he needs while he's there.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. I have one final question:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided, even though Alex has been in hospital for about 3 months (which is at the 12-week threshold where eligibility can be affected), you qualify for an important exception. 

Because you regularly spend money on Alex while he's in hospital - buying him clothes, toys, magazines, and other things he needs - you can continue to receive Child Benefit for him. This regular financial contribution towards his needs means you remain eligible despite the length of his hospital stay.

You also meet all the other key requirements:
- You live in the UK
- Alex is under 16 (he's 6 years old)
- You were responsible for Alex before he went into hospital (he lived with you)
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him

I hope Alex recovers soon and is able to come home to you.

reasonings: Alex is a 6-year-old child who lived with the claimant before going into hospital. According to the rules, Child Benefit payments might be affected if a child goes into hospital for more than 12 weeks. Alex has been in hospital for approximately 3 months (12 weeks), which is at the threshold. However, there is an exception to the rule: for children in a UK hospital, the claimant can continue to receive Child Benefit if they regularly spend money on the child. The claimant has confirmed they regularly spend money on Alex by buying him clothes, toys, magazines, and other things he needs while in hospital. This regular expenditure qualifies as an exception, allowing the claimant to remain eligible for Child Benefit for Alex. Additionally, the claimant lives in the UK, Alex lived with them before hospitalization (establishing responsibility), and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 6-year-old son Alex. Although Alex has been in hospital for approximately 3 months (at the 12-week threshold where eligibility might normally be affected), the claimant qualifies for an exception because they regularly spend money on Alex while he is in hospital. The claimant meets all other eligibility criteria: they live in the UK, Alex is under 16, the claimant was responsible for Alex before hospitalization (he lived with them), and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.
